response.justification: Bogdan is fundamentally a web and TypeScript/Node.js engineer with a rich distributed-systems background, but the role demands deep, production-grade C++ or Rust skills and direct experience with routing data pipelines and navigation tile generation — none of which he demonstrates. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, both over a decade old. His GIS work through GISCollective and Green Map is web-application focused (Node.js, Ember.js), not the geospatial data transformation and routing tile optimization the position requires. The experience gap on the primary technical axes is too wide to bridge at the senior level.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective and Green Map, including presenting at FOSS4G 2019
Demonstrated high-performance and low-latency distributed systems thinking at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers)
Broad cross-stack ownership and strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, Kubernetes)
concerns: C++ experience is sparse and over ten years old; no Rust listed anywhere; primary production language is TypeScript/Node.js
No experience designing or operating routing data pipelines, routing tile generation, or navigation guidance systems
GIS work is web-application layer (mapping UIs, event-driven data collection), not geospatial data transformation at pipeline scale
